behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so downstream summary can attribute statements to attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: mocked STT responses can always include perfect speaker tags and miss real diarization failures. REAL-INFRA: real API call is involved. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model-produced diarization quality, which can vary by audio/sample.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real diarized meeting audio through the real speech-to-text provider, then verify transcript utterances include stable speaker labels and that those labels map through to attendee attributions in produced summary fields.

behaviors.description: Structured summary includes at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: template/mocked LLM outputs can satisfy coverage trivially and hide extraction misses. LLM-DEP: this is semantic assignment detection and action-item generation quality from transcript content.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real transcript fixture with explicit task assignments per attendee, invoke the real LLM summarization step, and assert each assigned attendee appears in action_items with at least one corresponding task entry.

behaviors.description: Key decisions in transcript appear in the dedicated decisions section, not only narrative text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: mocked structured outputs won’t expose model misclassification between narrative vs decisions buckets. LLM-DEP: requires semantic identification and correct section placement by the model.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a transcript containing clearly stated decisions to the real summarization model and assert those decision statements are present in summary.decisions (and not exclusively in freeform narrative fields).

behaviors.description: Audio longer than 60 minutes is split, processed sequentially, and merged into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unit tests on splitter alone miss orchestration defects across splitter, segment queue, sequential executor, and merger. REAL-INFRA is not strictly required to catch core boundary/order/merge bugs. LLM-DEP is secondary if using deterministic test doubles for STT/summary content markers.
behaviors.test_description: With local filesystem fixtures, run a 60+ minute synthetic audio/test manifest through real segmentation + orchestration + merge modules using deterministic STT/LLM stubs per segment; assert no segment loss/duplication, strict sequential processing order, and merged output contains ordered contributions from all segments.

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes automatically for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real upload path, networked APIs, and delivery path with no manual steps. MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks can hide auth, timeout, payload-size, retry, and callback/serialization failures. Multi-step exists, but the primary boundary is full external-system E2E execution rather than agent-session logic validation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run in a staging environment using a real meeting audio file: upload via production-like entrypoint, call real STT and summary services, and verify final structured artifact is delivered to the configured sink within SLA with no human intervention.
